articles.title: Hong Kong Book Fair Opens Its Doors to Thousands of Readers
articles.summary: The Hong Kong Book Fair has returned to the Hong Kong Convention and Exhibition Centre, drawing large crowds of visitors eager to browse books, meet authors, and attend cultural events. The annual fair is one of the biggest literary gatherings in Asia, attracting both local residents and tourists. This year's event features hundreds of publishers from Hong Kong and around the world. Students and families are among the most enthusiastic visitors, with many queuing up early to grab discounted books. Organisers hope the fair will inspire a love of reading among young people and promote the city's vibrant publishing culture during the summer holidays.

lv1: What event is being held at the Hong Kong Convention and Exhibition Centre? (香港會議展覽中心正舉行什麼活動？)
Who are some of the most enthusiastic visitors at the Book Fair? (書展最踴躍的參觀者是哪些人？)
lv2: Why do you think the Hong Kong Book Fair is considered an important cultural event for the city? (你認為香港書展為何被視為城市的重要文化活動？)
How might attending a book fair encourage young people to read more? (參加書展如何鼓勵年輕人多閱讀？)
lv3: In an age of smartphones and online content, do physical book fairs still serve an important purpose? Give reasons for your view. (在智能手機和網絡內容盛行的時代，實體書展是否仍有重要意義？請說明你的看法。)
Some people say books should be freely available online to everyone. Do you agree or disagree, and what impact might this have on publishers and authors? (有人認為書籍應在網上免費供所有人閱覽，你同意嗎？這對出版商和作者有何影響？)

articles.title: Hong Kong Tech Industry Sets Goal to Develop New Industries Within Five Years
articles.summary: Hong Kong's Productivity Council has announced that the city's emerging technology industries are expected to achieve significant research and development milestones within the next five years. Officials say sectors including artificial intelligence, biotechnology, and green energy are among the key areas being targeted for growth. The council believes that investing in innovation now will help Hong Kong strengthen its position as a global technology hub. Schools and universities are also being encouraged to prepare students with the skills needed for these future industries. The announcement reflects the government's broader ambition to transform Hong Kong into a leading smart city in Asia.

lv1: Which organisation announced the new technology development goals for Hong Kong? (哪個機構宣布了香港的新科技發展目標？)
Name one technology sector that Hong Kong wants to develop. (說出一個香港希望發展的科技領域。)
lv2: Why is it important for Hong Kong to invest in emerging technology industries? (為什麼香港投資新興科技產業很重要？)
How can schools help prepare students for jobs in technology and innovation? (學校如何幫助學生為科技及創新行業做好準備？)
lv3: Should the government spend more public money on technology research, or should this be left to private companies? Explain your thinking. (政府應否花更多公共資金於科技研究，還是應留給私人企業負責？請解釋你的想法。)
If Hong Kong becomes a major technology hub, what benefits and challenges might this bring to ordinary residents? (如果香港成為主要科技中心，對普通市民有什麼好處和挑戰？)

articles.title: 宏福苑大火調查委員會展開第五輪聆訊　專家證人出庭作供
articles.summary: 大埔宏福苑大火獨立調查委員會今日召開第五輪聆訊，分別邀請事實證人及專家證人到庭陳述。委員會首席代表資深大律師引述專家報告，就火災起因及相關政府部門的應對安排進行深入審查。調查旨在找出事故成因，並為日後防火措施提供改善建議，保障市民居住安全。委員會的調查結果預計將對本港公共房屋的消防安全政策產生深遠影響，令社會各界高度關注。

lv1: 宏福苑大火獨立委員會今日展開第幾輪聆訊？
委員會召開聆訊的主要目的是什麼？
lv2: 為什麼調查委員會需要邀請專家證人出庭作供？
宏福苑大火調查的結果，對香港公共房屋的消防安全有什麼潛在影響？
lv3: 你認為政府、房屋管理機構及居民各自應負起哪些責任，才能有效預防住宅火災？
獨立調查委員會相比由政府部門自行調查，有什麼優點和缺點？

articles.title: 聯合國報告警告：人工智能數據中心耗水量急增　威脅全球供水
articles.summary: 聯合國大學水與環境健康研究所發表最新報告，指出隨著人工智能（AI）技術迅速普及，全球各地的數據中心為冷卻電腦伺服器需要消耗大量清潔食水，對全球淡水資源構成嚴重威脅。報告警告，若各地政府及科技企業不盡快採取節水措施及研發更環保的冷卻技術，未來水資源短缺問題將進一步惡化。此議題對水資源本已緊張的亞洲地區尤其值得關注，社會各界應積極思考AI發展與環境保護之間的平衡。

lv1: 根據聯合國報告，數據中心為什麼需要消耗大量食水？
哪個機構發表了這份關於人工智能與食水的報告？
lv2: 為什麼人工智能技術的普及會加劇全球水資源短缺的問題？
科技企業可以採取哪些措施，減少數據中心的耗水量？
lv3: 人工智能為社會帶來很多好處，但同時對環境造成負擔，你認為我們應如何在科技發展與環境保護之間取得平衡？
若政府規定科技公司必須公開其數據中心的耗水量及碳排放數據，你認為這是否合理？為什麼？
